hypothesis: If a generative model undergoes transfer learning by pre-training on large chemical databases (ChEMBL and ZINC-15) and is fine-tuned on a curated Covalent Triazine Framework dataset with reinforcement-learning reward functions encoding expert criteria, then the produced porous carbon structures will show higher validity, uniqueness, internal diversity, novelty, and percentage passing expert filters, and lower Fréchet ChemNet Distance, than models lacking this transfer-learning and RL setup, because pre-training and reward shaping bias the models toward chemically feasible, symmetry- and conjugation-aware, design-aligned CTF candidates.
